title: abrotanella patearoa
intro: Abrotanella patearoa, also known as Maire's abrotanella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of Abrotanella patearoa have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Abrotanella patearoa is often dried and made into a tea or infusion to take advantage of its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and promoting relaxation.
